Marvin Phillip.bio: One of the most experienced goalkeepers in Caribbean football history, Marvin Phillip has been the backbone of Trinidad and Tobago's goalkeeping department for well over a decade. Starting his career as a striker at schoolboy level before converting to goalkeeper, Phillip has earned over 100 caps for the Soca Warriors, served as captain on numerous occasions, and featured in four CONCACAF Gold Cups. Named W Connection's Goalkeeper of the Year in 2007, he won the Caribbean Club Championship with Connection in 2008-09 before later playing for Central FC and Defence Force in the TT Pro League.
Marvin Phillip.playingStyle: Experienced, commanding goalkeeper with excellent shot-stopping reflexes, strong penalty-area presence, vocal leadership qualities, and the tactical intelligence gained from nearly two decades of professional football
keyStrengths: Shot-stopping ability honed over a 20-year career
Vocal leadership and defensive organization
Penalty-saving record and composure in high-pressure moments
Marvin Phillip.worldCup2026Role: Veteran first-choice goalkeeper whose century of international caps provides Trinidad and Tobago with unmatched experience and calm between the posts in their World Cup campaign
Marvin Phillip.notableFact: Originally played as a striker at schoolboy level before retraining as a goalkeeper, going on to become one of the most capped goalkeepers in Trinidad and Tobago history with over 100 international appearances across four Gold Cups

Denzil Smith.bio: A rising goalkeeper who has earned his place through impressive performances in CONCACAF Nations League and World Cup qualifying matches. A product of the Shiva Boys HC youth system, Smith developed through the TT Premier Football League with W Connection, Club Sando, and AC Port of Spain before making the move to USL League One side AV Alta FC in January 2025, becoming the club's first goalkeeper signing. He has recorded six clean sheets in 16 senior international appearances since his first call-up in March 2023.
Denzil Smith.playingStyle: Athletic, modern goalkeeper with quick reflexes, good shot-stopping ability, and the confidence to command his penalty area during set pieces and aerial challenges
keyStrengths: Clean sheet record - six in 16 international appearances
Quick reflexes and shot-stopping at close range
Confidence in commanding the penalty area
Denzil Smith.worldCup2026Role: Reliable backup goalkeeper who has proven himself at the international level; his six clean sheets in 16 caps demonstrate he can step in without any drop-off in quality if called upon during the tournament
Denzil Smith.notableFact: Recorded an impressive six clean sheets in just 16 senior international appearances for Trinidad and Tobago across CONCACAF Nations League, World Cup qualifying, and international friendlies since his first call-up in March 2023

Jabari St Hillaire.bio: A tall, imposing goalkeeper who has risen through domestic Trinidadian football to earn his place in the national team setup. Standing at 195cm, St Hillaire developed with La Horquetta Rangers, winning the Ascension Invitational Tournament Division 1 in 2022, before joining Defence Force where he helped the club win the TT Premier League title in 2024-25 and the League Cup in 2025. His impressive shot-stopping and penalty-saving abilities have caught the eye of national team selectors.
Jabari St Hillaire.playingStyle: Tall, commanding goalkeeper who uses his impressive height and wingspan to dominate his penalty area, with strong reflexes and proven ability to save penalties in crucial domestic matches
keyStrengths: Imposing physical presence at 195cm
Penalty-saving ability in high-pressure situations
Domestic trophy-winning experience with Defence Force
Jabari St Hillaire.worldCup2026Role: Third-choice goalkeeper providing reliable depth; his imposing frame, domestic trophy-winning pedigree, and growing experience make him a valuable squad member who can be called upon if needed
Jabari St Hillaire.notableFact: Won the TT Premier League and League Cup with Defence Force and saved a penalty from veteran striker Keron Cummings in a crucial domestic match, showcasing his composure under pressure

Sheldon Bateau.bio: A well-traveled center-back who has gained invaluable experience across European football, playing in Belgium with KV Mechelen, in Kazakhstan with FC Kairat, in Russia with Krylia Sovetov Samara, in Norway with Sarpsborg 08, and in Turkey with Samsunspor. Born in Port of Spain, Bateau represented Trinidad and Tobago at under-17, under-20, and under-23 Olympic level before becoming a mainstay of the senior squad, earning over 60 caps and scoring 5 goals from defense since his debut in February 2013 against Peru.
Sheldon Bateau.playingStyle: Experienced, physically strong center-back who combines aerial dominance with composure on the ball, capable of stepping forward to contribute goals from set pieces while maintaining defensive discipline developed across five European leagues
keyStrengths: Extensive European club experience across five countries
Aerial ability and goal threat from set pieces
Composure and tactical awareness from diverse football cultures
Sheldon Bateau.worldCup2026Role: Experienced center-back whose European pedigree across Belgium, Kazakhstan, Russia, Norway, and Turkey gives Trinidad and Tobago a defender who has faced a wide variety of playing styles and tactical systems at the World Cup level
Sheldon Bateau.notableFact: Has played professional football in Belgium, Kazakhstan, Russia, Norway, and Turkey, making him one of the most widely traveled players in Trinidad and Tobago football history with experience across five European countries

Aubrey David.bio: A veteran defender and one of Trinidad and Tobago's most reliable center-backs over the past decade, Aubrey David has amassed over 85 international caps and appeared in five consecutive CONCACAF Gold Cup tournaments from 2015 to 2025. Born in Georgetown, Guyana, David committed to Trinidad and Tobago at senior level and has built a remarkable club career spanning Finland, Kazakhstan, Costa Rica with Deportivo Saprissa, the United States with FC Dallas, Ecuador, and Guatemala with CSD Municipal, accumulating over 475 career club appearances.
Aubrey David.playingStyle: Commanding, experienced center-back who reads the game intelligently, positions himself well to intercept danger, and provides defensive leadership through communication and organizational ability forged across seven countries
keyStrengths: Exceptional international experience with 85+ caps
Defensive reading of the game and positioning
Leadership and communication as an occasional captain
Aubrey David.worldCup2026Role: Veteran defensive leader and one of the most experienced players in the squad; his 85+ caps and five Gold Cup campaigns provide irreplaceable leadership at the heart of the Trinidad and Tobago defense
Aubrey David.notableFact: Has represented Trinidad and Tobago in five consecutive CONCACAF Gold Cup tournaments from 2015 to 2025, amassing over 475 career club appearances across seven different countries including a loan spell at FC Dallas in MLS

Kobi Henry.bio: One of Trinidad and Tobago's most exciting young defensive talents, Kobi Henry is a center-back who developed in the Stade de Reims academy in France before earning a permanent move to MLS side Real Salt Lake on a two-year deal in December 2025. Born in Florida to Trinidadian parents, Henry made his international debut in September 2024 against Curacao and immediately became an automatic selection, starting all six matches in the final round of 2026 World Cup qualification. His composure on the ball and tactical intelligence belie his young age.
Kobi Henry.playingStyle: Modern, ball-playing center-back with excellent composure in possession, strong tactical intelligence developed in the French academy system, and the athleticism to recover in one-on-one defensive situations
keyStrengths: Ball-playing ability and composure from the back
Tactical intelligence from French academy development at Stade de Reims
Athletic recovery speed and one-on-one defending
Kobi Henry.worldCup2026Role: Starting center-back and one of the squad's most promising young talents; his ability to build from the back and his immediate integration into the national team make him central to Trinidad and Tobago's defensive plans at the World Cup
Kobi Henry.notableFact: Developed in the Stade de Reims academy in France, played on loan at FC Villefranche, and signed permanently with Real Salt Lake where he started all six World Cup qualifying matches for Trinidad and Tobago after making his debut in September 2024

Josiah Trimmingham.bio: A physically imposing center-back nicknamed 'Tank' by his teammates for his robust defensive style, Josiah Trimmingham has built a career through sheer determination and physicality. After starting with San Juan Jabloteh in the TT Pro League in 2014, Trimmingham earned a move to Forward Madison in USL League One, where he led the team with 70 clearances and 37 aerial duels won during the 2020 season. He has also played for Club Sando and in the Jamaica Premier League with Montego Bay United, earning 18 international caps since his debut in 2017.
Josiah Trimmingham.playingStyle: Physically dominant, aggressive center-back who excels in aerial duels, clearances, and physical confrontations, using his imposing frame at 188cm and 95kg to win the majority of defensive challenges
keyStrengths: Aerial dominance and physical presence at 95kg
Aggressive tackling and clearance ability
Set-piece threat from corners and free kicks
Josiah Trimmingham.worldCup2026Role: Physical defensive option who provides aerial dominance and set-piece threat; his nickname 'Tank' reflects the uncompromising style he brings to the Trinidad and Tobago backline against any opposition
Josiah Trimmingham.notableFact: Earned the nickname 'Tank' from teammates at Forward Madison for his physical style of play, leading the team with 70 clearances and 37 aerial duels won in a single USL League One season

Alvin Jones.bio: A household name in CONCACAF football, Alvin Jones is the younger brother of fellow Soca Warrior Joevin Jones and the son of former Trinidad and Tobago international Kelvin Jones. A versatile and tenacious right-back, Alvin earned international fame on October 10, 2017, when his stunning long-range strike against the United States helped Trinidad and Tobago inflict a historic 2-1 defeat that eliminated the Americans from 2018 World Cup qualification. He has earned over 65 caps, scored 6 international goals, and featured in three consecutive Gold Cup campaigns in 2019, 2021, and 2023.
Alvin Jones.playingStyle: Energetic, attack-minded right-back who combines aggressive overlapping runs with a powerful long-range shot, while providing tenacious defensive work and versatility to play across the backline
keyStrengths: Powerful long-range shooting ability
Attacking overlaps and tenacious defending
Versatility across defensive positions
Alvin Jones.worldCup2026Role: Experienced right-back and fan favorite whose big-game pedigree and attacking threat from deep make him a crucial part of Trinidad and Tobago's defensive setup and devastating counter-attacking play
Alvin Jones.notableFact: Scored one of the most famous goals in CONCACAF history - a stunning long-range strike against the United States on October 10, 2017, that helped eliminate the Americans from 2018 World Cup qualification in a historic 2-1 victory

Rio Cardines.bio: The youngest player in the Trinidad and Tobago squad at just 19 years old, Rio Cardines is a prodigiously talented fullback developing at Crystal Palace in the English Premier League. Born in Stevenage, England to Trinidadian parents, Cardines signed his professional contract with Crystal Palace in July 2024 and made his first-team debut as a substitute in a Europa Conference League match against Dynamo Kyiv in October 2025. He was part of the Crystal Palace squad that won the 2025 Community Shield against Liverpool and has started three consecutive World Cup qualifying matches for Trinidad and Tobago.
Rio Cardines.playingStyle: Dynamic, technically gifted young fullback with excellent pace, modern attacking instincts developed through Crystal Palace's Premier League academy, and defensive awareness that belies his teenage years
keyStrengths: Premier League academy development and technical quality
Pace and attacking drive from the fullback position
Rapid adaptation to senior international football
Rio Cardines.worldCup2026Role: Exciting young fullback whose Premier League pedigree at Crystal Palace makes him one of Trinidad and Tobago's most valuable assets; his ability to compete at the highest club level translates directly to World Cup football
Rio Cardines.notableFact: Made his Crystal Palace first-team debut in the Europa Conference League against Dynamo Kyiv at just 19 and was part of the squad that won the 2025 Community Shield against Liverpool, making him the highest-profile club player in the Trinidad and Tobago squad

Joevin Jones.bio: The most capped player in the current Trinidad and Tobago squad with over 100 international appearances and 14 goals from left-back, Joevin Jones is one of the greatest players in Soca Warriors history. The elder brother of Alvin Jones and son of former international Kelvin Jones, Joevin built his reputation through productive spells in MLS with the Chicago Fire and Seattle Sounders, winning two MLS Cups with Seattle. He also played in Germany's 2. Bundesliga with SV Darmstadt 98 and won the Finnish Veikkausliiga with HJK Helsinki on loan. His 14 international goals make him one of the most attack-minded defenders in CONCACAF history.
Joevin Jones.playingStyle: Attack-minded left-back with exceptional crossing ability, pace to overlap effectively, and the rare ability to score goals regularly from the fullback position, backed by the tactical discipline developed in MLS and the Bundesliga
keyStrengths: Over 100 international caps and exceptional experience
Prolific goal-scoring record from left-back with 14 international goals
Two-time MLS Cup winner with Seattle Sounders
Joevin Jones.worldCup2026Role: Veteran captain and the squad's most experienced player; his 100+ caps, MLS Cup-winning pedigree, and unique attacking ability from left-back make him the heartbeat of Trinidad and Tobago's team at the World Cup
Joevin Jones.notableFact: Won two MLS Cups with the Seattle Sounders, played in Germany's 2. Bundesliga with Darmstadt, scored 14 international goals from left-back, and follows in the footsteps of his father Kelvin as a Trinidad and Tobago international

Khaleem Hyland.bio: A veteran defensive midfielder who spent the best years of his career in Belgian football, Khaleem Hyland has been one of Trinidad and Tobago's most important midfielders for over 15 years. Born in Carenage, he began with San Juan Jabloteh before moving to Belgium in 2009, first with Zulte Waregem on loan then permanently, before signing with KRC Genk in August 2011. With over 90 international caps since his debut in January 2008, Hyland nearly joined Celtic before the transfer fell through, a disappointment he has spoken publicly about.
Khaleem Hyland.playingStyle: Disciplined, combative defensive midfielder who shields the back four effectively, breaks up opposition attacks through intelligent interceptions and fierce tackling, and provides a stable platform for the creative players ahead
keyStrengths: Tactical discipline and positional intelligence from Belgian Pro League experience
Fierce tackling and ball-winning ability
Over 90 caps of international experience
Khaleem Hyland.worldCup2026Role: Veteran midfield anchor whose extensive Belgian Pro League experience with Zulte Waregem and KRC Genk and 90+ caps provide Trinidad and Tobago with invaluable know-how in controlling the tempo and protecting the defense
Khaleem Hyland.notableFact: Spent the majority of his European career in Belgium's top flight with Zulte Waregem and KRC Genk, and has publicly spoken about the emotional disappointment of a near-move to Celtic that ultimately fell through

Daniel Phillips.bio: A talented young midfielder who came through the Chelsea academy from under-10 to under-14 level before developing at Watford, Daniel Phillips represents the new generation of dual-heritage players choosing to represent Trinidad and Tobago. Born in Enfield, London to a family of Trinidadian descent, Phillips made his professional debut for Watford in a Carabao Cup match against Oxford United in September 2020. After loan spells at Gillingham and two seasons at St Johnstone in the Scottish Premiership, he joined Stevenage in League One in July 2024, making 42 appearances in his first season.
Daniel Phillips.playingStyle: Technically proficient central midfielder with good passing range, intelligent movement off the ball, and the tactical awareness and competitive edge developed through the Chelsea and Watford academy systems
keyStrengths: Technical quality developed in Chelsea's academy
Tactical intelligence and passing accuracy
Experience across multiple British leagues - English, Scottish
Daniel Phillips.worldCup2026Role: Key central midfielder whose English football education at Chelsea and Watford gives Trinidad and Tobago a technically sound option who can control possession and dictate tempo against World Cup-level opposition
Daniel Phillips.notableFact: Developed in the Chelsea academy from under-10 to under-14 before moving to Watford, and made 42 appearances for Stevenage in League One in his first season, establishing himself as one of the club's most consistent performers

Andre Rampersad.bio: Known affectionately as 'Mr. Halifax,' Andre Rampersad is the captain and all-time record appearance holder for Canadian Premier League club HFX Wanderers, where he has been since the league's inaugural season in 2019. Growing up in the La Horquetta neighborhood of Arima, Trinidad and Tobago, Rampersad originally played cricket before switching to football at age 12 and developing through FC Santa Rosa, winning the 2018 TT Super League title. He holds the Wanderers franchise record with 158+ matches and signed a contract extension through 2026.
Andre Rampersad.playingStyle: Tireless, box-to-box midfielder who leads by example with relentless pressing, intelligent positioning, and the ability to cover vast areas of the pitch while contributing at both ends of the field
keyStrengths: Leadership and captaincy qualities
Tireless work rate and pitch coverage
All-time record appearance holder with 158+ matches for HFX Wanderers
Andre Rampersad.worldCup2026Role: Hardworking midfield option who brings leadership, energy, and tenacity; his captaincy experience and record-breaking longevity demonstrate the character and commitment he would bring to World Cup matches
Andre Rampersad.notableFact: Holds the all-time appearance record for HFX Wanderers with 158+ matches and is nicknamed 'Mr. Halifax' - originally played cricket before switching to football at age 12 in the La Horquetta neighborhood of Trinidad

Wayne Frederick II.bio: A young, dual-national midfielder who was selected with the second overall pick in the 2024 MLS SuperDraft by the Colorado Rapids after two standout seasons at Duke University. Born to a Trinidadian family, Frederick made his international debut in December 2024 in a friendly against Saudi Arabia at just 20 years old and has quickly become an important part of the national team setup, earning call-ups for the CONCACAF Gold Cup preliminary rounds and World Cup qualifying campaigns. He has adapted to playing midfield after initially being deployed in a different position at Duke.
Wayne Frederick II.playingStyle: Athletic, versatile young midfielder with good physical attributes, the ability to play multiple midfield positions, and a rapidly developing game shaped by MLS coaching and international experience
keyStrengths: Athletic ability and physical presence
Versatility across midfield positions
Second overall MLS SuperDraft selection pedigree
Wayne Frederick II.worldCup2026Role: Exciting young midfielder whose second-overall MLS SuperDraft pedigree and rapid integration into the national team signal his importance to Trinidad and Tobago's present and future midfield aspirations at the World Cup
Wayne Frederick II.notableFact: Was selected second overall in the 2024 MLS SuperDraft by the Colorado Rapids after starring at Duke University, making his Trinidad and Tobago debut at just 20 years old against Saudi Arabia in December 2024

Duane Muckette.bio: A creative midfielder who has been one of Trinidad and Tobago's most promising playmakers over the past decade, Duane Muckette developed through the national youth ranks before playing college soccer at the University of South Florida in the United States. His career has taken him from Portugal with Barreirense to the USL Championship with Memphis 901 FC during their inaugural season in 2019, and he has served as captain of AC Port of Spain in the TT Premier Football League, leading them to a runners-up finish in the inaugural edition of the competition.
Duane Muckette.playingStyle: Creative, technically gifted attacking midfielder with excellent vision, precise passing in the final third, and the ability to unlock defenses with incisive through balls and clever movement between the lines
keyStrengths: Creative vision and final-third passing
Technical quality developed across multiple football cultures
Leadership experience as club captain at AC Port of Spain
Duane Muckette.worldCup2026Role: Creative midfield option who can unlock opposition defenses with his vision and passing; his experience across American, European, and Caribbean football demonstrates his adaptability for the World Cup stage
Duane Muckette.notableFact: Captained AC Port of Spain to a runners-up finish in the inaugural TT Premier Football League, played college soccer at the University of South Florida, and was part of Memphis 901 FC's inaugural USL Championship squad in 2019

Kevin Molino.bio: The most decorated MLS player in Trinidad and Tobago history, Kevin Molino is a supremely talented attacking midfielder who lit up Major League Soccer during prolific spells with Orlando City and Minnesota United. His record-breaking 20-goal USL season with Orlando in 2014 announced his arrival, while his 13-goal 2020 MLS campaign with Minnesota - more than any other winger in the league that year - cemented his status. After MLS stints with Orlando, Minnesota United, and Columbus Crew, Molino returned home in October 2024 to captain Defence Force in the TT Pro League, bringing 26 international goals and 70 caps to the national team.
Kevin Molino.playingStyle: Skilful, creative attacking midfielder with exceptional dribbling ability, clinical finishing from inside and outside the box, and the vision to create chances with incisive passes and clever movement as the iconic number 10
keyStrengths: Clinical goal-scoring ability with 26 international goals
Exceptional dribbling and close control
MLS pedigree with over 200 career appearances across Orlando, Minnesota, and Columbus
Kevin Molino.worldCup2026Role: Veteran creative force and the squad's iconic number 10; his 26 international goals, MLS pedigree, and ability to produce moments of individual brilliance make him Trinidad and Tobago's most dangerous creative weapon at the World Cup
Kevin Molino.notableFact: Scored a USL-record 20 goals in the 2014 regular season with Orlando City, later became the most productive winger in MLS during the 2020 season with 13 goals for Minnesota United, and returned home to captain Defence Force in 2024

Ajani Fortune.bio: A dynamic young attacking midfielder who rose through the Atlanta United academy to become one of MLS's most exciting young talents, Ajani 'Jay' Fortune represents the bright future of Trinidad and Tobago football. Born in the United States, Fortune joined the Atlanta United Academy in 2018, made his professional debut for Atlanta United 2 at just 17, and became only the 14th player in franchise history to earn a first-team contract from the academy. He received his first Trinidad and Tobago senior call-up at 18 years old and has scored key goals in CONCACAF competition.
Ajani Fortune.playingStyle: Dynamic, energetic attacking midfielder who combines pace, creative passing, and goal-scoring ability with the tactical discipline and work rate instilled through Atlanta United's demanding academy system
keyStrengths: Pace, energy, and dynamic attacking movement
Goal-scoring ability from midfield positions
MLS first-team experience developed through elite academy pathway
Ajani Fortune.worldCup2026Role: Exciting young attacking talent who can change games with his pace and creativity; his MLS experience and ability to contribute goals from midfield give Trinidad and Tobago a potent option in the final third
Ajani Fortune.notableFact: Became only the 14th player in Atlanta United history to transition from the academy to the first team, received his first Trinidad and Tobago senior call-up at just 18 years old in January 2021, and scored on his Gold Cup debut against Saint Kitts and Nevis

Levi Garcia.bio: Trinidad and Tobago's most valuable and high-profile player, Levi Garcia is a dynamic forward who became the most expensive signing in Spartak Moscow's history when he joined the Russian club for a record 18.7 million euros in February 2025. Garcia's remarkable career trajectory took him from T&TEC in the TT Pro League at just 15 years old to Central FC, then to Israel, and on to AEK Athens where he scored 56 goals in 160 appearances, winning the Greek Super League and Greek Cup double in 2022-23. With 50 caps and 14 goals, he is the Soca Warriors' most dangerous attacking weapon.
Levi Garcia.playingStyle: Explosive, direct forward with blistering pace, excellent dribbling ability, clinical finishing, and the versatility to play across the front line while consistently threatening opposition defenses with movement and power
keyStrengths: Pace, directness, and one-on-one ability against defenders
Prolific goal-scoring record with 56 goals in 160 appearances at AEK Athens
Record-breaking 18.7 million euro transfer demonstrating elite quality
Levi Garcia.worldCup2026Role: Undisputed star player and Trinidad and Tobago's primary attacking weapon; his record transfer to Spartak Moscow and proven goal-scoring pedigree in European football make him the focal point of every attack at the World Cup
Levi Garcia.notableFact: Became the most expensive signing in Spartak Moscow's 103-year history at 18.7 million euros in February 2025, having scored 56 goals in 160 appearances for AEK Athens and won the Greek Super League and Cup double in 2022-23

Real Gill.bio: A versatile attacker who can play anywhere across the front three, Real Gill has built his career through the American soccer pyramid while establishing himself as a regular in the Trinidad and Tobago national team. After impressing with Northern Colorado Hailstorm in USL League One, where he helped win the 2024 USL Jagermeister Cup, Gill moved to MLS NEXT Pro side Huntsville City. His international career began with a debut in May 2022, and he has appeared in CONCACAF Nations League, two Gold Cups, and World Cup qualifying, scoring his first goal in a 2-0 victory over Saint Martin in January 2023.
Real Gill.playingStyle: Versatile, exciting attacking player who can operate across all three positions in the front line, bringing pace, creativity, and a fearless, crowd-pleasing approach to taking on defenders
keyStrengths: Versatility across the forward line - can play any attacking position
Pace and directness in one-on-one situations
Big-game international experience across three CONCACAF competitions
Real Gill.worldCup2026Role: Versatile attacking option who provides tactical flexibility across the front three; his ability to play multiple positions and his fearless approach give the coaching staff valuable options from the bench or in rotation
Real Gill.notableFact: Won the 2024 USL Jagermeister Cup with Northern Colorado Hailstorm and has represented Trinidad and Tobago across three different CONCACAF competitions - Nations League, Gold Cup, and World Cup qualifying - since debuting in 2022

Nathaniel James.bio: A dynamic young winger who won the TT Premier Football League Golden Boot in 2023 with 15 goals in 22 appearances for Club Sando, Nathaniel James has quickly risen through the ranks of Trinidad and Tobago football. After impressing domestically, James moved to Jamaica's Mount Pleasant Football Academy before signing with USL League One side Portland Hearts of Pine, where he helped the club reach the league semifinal in their inaugural season and won the fan vote for the 2025 USL League One Goal of the Year. He then signed with North Texas SC on an MLS NEXT Pro contract through 2026.
Nathaniel James.playingStyle: Quick, dynamic winger with excellent finishing ability, creative dribbling skills, and the confidence to take on defenders and produce moments of individual brilliance in the final third
keyStrengths: Goal-scoring ability from wide positions - Golden Boot winner
Pace and direct dribbling to beat defenders
Adaptability proven across Trinidad, Jamaica, and the United States
Nathaniel James.worldCup2026Role: Exciting wide attacking option whose goal-scoring instincts from the wing add a crucial dimension; his 4 goals in 12 caps and Golden Boot-winning domestic form make him a dangerous squad member at the World Cup
Nathaniel James.notableFact: Won the TT Premier Football League Golden Boot in 2023 with 15 goals in 22 appearances for Club Sando, and later won the fan vote for the 2025 USL League One Goal of the Year with Portland Hearts of Pine

Judah Garcia.bio: Part of a remarkable football family that includes brothers Daniel and Nathaniel, Judah Garcia is a versatile attacker who has gained experience across three continents. Born in Santa Flora, Trinidad, Garcia played in India's I-League with NEROCA, scoring 3 goals in 12 league matches, before joining AEK Athens B in Greece on a three-year contract in August 2021. He currently plays for FK Partizani in the Albanian Superliga, continuing to develop his game in European football while contributing 20 caps and 3 goals to the Trinidad and Tobago national team.
Judah Garcia.playingStyle: Versatile, hardworking attacker who can play on either wing or through the middle, combining pace and directness with a strong work ethic and the ability to create chances for teammates in multiple systems
keyStrengths: Versatility across all attacking positions
Experience on three continents - Caribbean, Asia, and Europe
Work rate and team-first mentality
Judah Garcia.worldCup2026Role: Versatile attacking option whose experience in Indian and European football broadens Trinidad and Tobago's tactical options; his ability to play multiple positions across the front line provides valuable World Cup squad flexibility
Judah Garcia.notableFact: Part of a remarkable football family with brothers who all play professional football, and has played on three continents - from India's I-League with NEROCA to Greece with AEK Athens B to Albania's Superliga with FK Partizani

Ryan Telfer.bio: A winger with a unique cross-cultural background, Ryan Telfer was born in Canada to Trinidadian parents, raised in Trinidad, then returned to Canada to forge his football career. He etched his name into Canadian football history by scoring the first-ever goal in Canadian Premier League history for York9 FC against Forge FC on April 27, 2019. His career has included spells with Toronto FC in MLS, where he scored a game-winning goal against Orlando City on his debut, as well as Miami FC in the USL Championship. He now plays for HFX Wanderers alongside teammate Andre Rampersad.
Ryan Telfer.playingStyle: Pacy, direct winger who thrives running at defenders with speed and trickery, delivering dangerous crosses and possessing the ability to cut inside and create scoring opportunities with both feet
keyStrengths: Pace and direct running at defenders
Crossing ability and chance creation from wide areas
Historical goal-scoring significance in Canadian football
Ryan Telfer.worldCup2026Role: Experienced wide attacker who can stretch opposition defenses with his pace and deliver quality service; his MLS experience and familiarity with North American conditions are valuable for a World Cup held across the continent
Ryan Telfer.notableFact: Scored the first goal in Canadian Premier League history for York9 FC against Forge FC on April 27, 2019, and previously scored on his MLS debut for Toronto FC with a game-winning goal against Orlando City

Tyrese Spicer.bio: An inspiring story of perseverance, Tyrese Spicer overcame a childhood of poverty in Trinidad and Tobago to become the number one overall pick in the 2024 MLS SuperDraft by Toronto FC. After a standout collegiate career at Lipscomb University where he was named ASUN Player of the Year in 2023 with 14 goals in 16 matches, Spicer was traded to Orlando City where he made an immediate impact, scoring in just one minute and ten seconds of his first start against Sporting Kansas City - the fastest goal in Orlando City history. He has earned international recognition with 8 caps and 2 goals.
Tyrese Spicer.playingStyle: Quick, powerful striker with electric pace, clinical finishing ability, and the hunger and determination forged through overcoming adversity that drives him to outwork every opponent on the pitch
keyStrengths: Electric pace and explosive acceleration
Clinical finishing - scored fastest goal in Orlando City history
Mental resilience and determination forged through overcoming poverty
Tyrese Spicer.worldCup2026Role: Dynamic attacking option whose MLS pedigree as the number one overall SuperDraft pick and clinical finishing ability give Trinidad and Tobago a potent goal threat capable of producing instant impact off the bench or from the start
Tyrese Spicer.notableFact: Was the number one overall pick in the 2024 MLS SuperDraft and scored the fastest goal in Orlando City history - one minute and ten seconds into his first start - after overcoming a childhood of poverty in Trinidad to reach the professional ranks

Dante Sealy.bio: A dual-national attacker who developed through the FC Dallas academy before spending two formative seasons on loan at PSV Eindhoven's under-21 team in the Netherlands, logging 57 matches with 6 goals in 2,734 minutes. After returning from Holland, Sealy had a career-best season at CF Montreal in 2025 before the Colorado Rapids acquired him for a club-record 1.9 million dollars in General Allocation Money in December 2025. He chose Trinidad and Tobago over the United States and made a spectacular debut in June 2025 with two goals against Saint Kitts and Nevis.
Dante Sealy.playingStyle: Left-footed, technically gifted forward who combines pace and creativity with clinical finishing, able to play as a winger or center forward with the tactical intelligence developed through Dutch and American football systems
keyStrengths: Left-footed technical quality and finishing
European development experience with PSV Eindhoven in the Netherlands
Versatility to play as a winger or center forward
Dante Sealy.worldCup2026Role: Exciting attacking talent whose European development at PSV Eindhoven and rising MLS career make him one of Trinidad and Tobago's most dangerous creative threats, capable of producing game-changing moments with his left foot
Dante Sealy.notableFact: Scored twice on his Trinidad and Tobago debut against Saint Kitts and Nevis, spent two seasons on loan at PSV Eindhoven logging 57 matches in the Netherlands, and was acquired by the Colorado Rapids for a club-record 1.9 million dollars in GAM

Justin Obikwu.bio: A powerful young striker born in England to a Nigerian-Trinidadian family who developed through the Coventry City academy. Standing at an imposing 194cm, Obikwu gained valuable experience on loan at Grimsby Town in League Two, scoring his first professional goal in a draw with Sutton United, and at Lincoln City in League One, where he scored against Luton Town in just his second appearance. He signed permanently with Championship club Queens Park Rangers in January 2026 for a reported 500,000 pounds and made his Trinidad and Tobago debut in March 2024 against Jamaica.
Justin Obikwu.playingStyle: Tall, powerful target striker who uses his imposing 194cm frame to dominate aerial duels, hold the ball up effectively for teammates, and create space through physical presence while possessing increasingly clinical finishing
keyStrengths: Imposing physical presence at 194cm and aerial dominance
Hold-up play and target-man qualities
English Championship experience and rapidly improving finishing
Justin Obikwu.worldCup2026Role: Physical attacking option who provides a different dimension with his aerial threat and target-man qualities; his EFL Championship experience with QPR and imposing frame make him an ideal plan-B option from the bench against any opposition
Justin Obikwu.notableFact: Signed for Championship club Queens Park Rangers for a reported 500,000 pounds in January 2026 after impressive loan spells at Grimsby Town and Lincoln City, having come through the Coventry City academy system

Malcolm Shaw.bio: A clinical striker born in Canada who chose to represent Trinidad and Tobago at international level, Malcolm Shaw built his reputation through four productive seasons with Atletico Ottawa in the Canadian Premier League, scoring 19 goals and providing 5 assists while helping the club reach the CPL Final in 2023. His consistent goal-scoring earned him a move to the League of Ireland Premier Division with Galway United in June 2025. Shaw has proven his quality at international level with an impressive strike rate of 3 goals in 8 caps, scoring in both CONCACAF Nations League and World Cup qualifying matches.
Malcolm Shaw.playingStyle: Clinical, intelligent striker with excellent movement inside the box, composed finishing, and the ability to score different types of goals while contributing to build-up play with neat link-up work around the penalty area
keyStrengths: Clinical finishing and composure in front of goal
Intelligent movement and positioning in the box
Proven international scoring record - 3 goals in 8 caps
Malcolm Shaw.worldCup2026Role: Reliable goal-scoring option whose impressive international strike rate of 3 goals in 8 caps and experience across Canadian and Irish football make him a valuable option when Trinidad and Tobago need a goal at the World Cup
Malcolm Shaw.notableFact: Scored 19 goals in four seasons with Atletico Ottawa in the Canadian Premier League, helped the club reach the CPL Final in 2023, and maintains an impressive international strike rate of nearly a goal every three games for Trinidad and Tobago
